revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ajika-jinja]][[ja:阿遅加神社]]
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py-->
{{Expand Japanese|阿遅加神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Ajika Shrine
| native_name = 阿遅加神社
| image = Ajika Jinja2008-2.jpg
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none
| map_type = Japan
| map_caption = Ajika Shrine
| coordinates = {{coord|35.350199|136.718265}}
| map_relief = 1
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru|arz|ياماتو تاكيرو|be|Ямата Такэру|de|Yamatotakeru|en|Yamato Takeru|es|Yamato Takeru|fa|یاماتو تاکه‌رو|fr|Yamato Takeru|hu|Jamatotakeru|id|Yamato Takeru|it|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|ko|야마토타케루|pt|Yamato Takeru|ru|Ямато Такэру|sh|Yamato Takeru|simple|Yamato Takeru|sq|Jamato Takeru|sr|Јамато Такеру|sv|Yamato Takeru|tr|Yamato Takeru|uk|Ямато Такеру|ur|یاماتو تاکیرو|vi|Yamato Takeru|zh_min_nan|Yamato Takeru|zh|日本武尊|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}
}}

{{nihongo|'''Ajika Shrine'''|阿遅加神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}

* {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}
* {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}}

== located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) ==

* {{ill|Hashima, Gifu|lt=Hashima|ar|هاشيما|azb|هاشیما|az|Haşima (şəhər)|bcl|Hashima, Gifu|ceb|Hashima (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Хасима (гӀала)|cs|Hašima (Gifu)|de|Hashima|en|Hashima, Gifu|eo|Hashima|es|Hashima (Gifu)|fa|هاشیما|fi|Hashima (Gifu)|fr|Hashima (ville)|gl|Hashima|it|Hashima|ja|羽島市|ko|하시마시|lt|Hašima|mzn|هاشیما، گیفو|nl|Hashima (stad)|pl|Hashima|pt|Hashima|ro|Hashima|ru|Хасима (город)|simple|Hashima, Gifu|su|Hashima, Gifu|sv|Hashima, Gifu|tg|Ҳашима|tl|Hashima, Gifu|tr|Hashima|tt|Хасима (шәһәр)|uk|Хасіма (Ґіфу)|vi|Hashima, Gifu|war|Hashima, Gifu|zh_classical|羽島市|zh_min_nan|Hasima-chhī|zh_yue|羽島|zh|羽島市|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}

ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah * 7200005003533

* Ajika-jinja

* {'text': '岐阜県羽島市足近町直道1088-2', 'language': 'ja'}








{{commons category|Category:Ajika-jinja}}
[[JA:阿遅加神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11657713}}

== Japanese content ==
{{Source citation| date = November 2024}}
{{Shinto shrine
 | Name=Ajika Shrine
 | Image=[[Image:Ajika Jinja2008-2.jpg|250px|]]
 | Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}} 1088-2 Nao-michi, Ashika-chō
 | Coordinates={{Wikicoordinates2|35|21|0.99|N|136|43|5.47|E|scale:25000}}
 | Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}
 | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | Founded=Unknown
 | Main hall=
 | Annual festival=Around October 10
 | Rituals=
 | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Ajika Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ajika Shrine''' (あぢかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}).

=== Overview ===
* After completing his eastern expedition and arriving in {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} headed for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} to defeat the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|qid=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. Learning of a sacred spring near the river marking the border between Owari and Mino (corresponding to the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} before {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|qid=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14); today’s {{ill|lt=Sakai River (Gifu Prefecture)|Q4330698|qid=Q4330698|lt=Sakai River|1=Sakai River (Gifu)}}), Yamato Takeru drank from the spring and rested for a time. The place where this sacred spring existed is said to be the site of Ajika Shrine.
* After Yamato Takeru died on his way back to {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, his son Wak Takehiko no Ō visited this place and built a shrine to enshrine Yamato Takeru, which is said to be the origin of the shrine.
* In {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|qid=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14), the Kiso River drastically changed its course due to a major flood. The border between Owari and Mino was shifted to the new course of the Kiso River, and the location of Ajika Shrine was changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|qid=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province.
* During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Waka no Miya,” “Hachiman Shrine,” and “Yatsurugi Daimyōjin.” In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the name was restored to Ajika Shrine.
* In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}
** There is also a theory that the enshrined deity is Mononobe no Ajiko no Muraji (the younger brother of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Arakabi|Q1114837|qid=Q1114837|1=Mononobe no Arakabi}}, the {{ill|lt=Ōmuraji|Q4328807|qid=Q4328807|lt=Ōmuraji|1=Ōmuraji}} who subdued the {{ill|lt=Iwai Rebellion|Q3138004|qid=Q3138004|1=Iwai Rebellion}}).

=== Location ===
* 1088-2 Nao-michi, Ashika-chō, Hashima City, Gifu Prefecture

=== Transportation ===
{{commonscat|Ajika-jinja}}
* Get off at the “Sakai” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Hashima City Community Bus|Q11610099|qid=Q11610099|1=Hashima City Community Bus}} Blue Bus line; 3-minute walk.
* About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Minami-Juku Station|Q11407722|qid=Q11407722|1=Minami-Juku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|Q901442|qid=Q901442|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}}.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

{{shinto-stub}}
